> **Authors:** Stefan Georgi (co-primary) & Justin Goff (co-primary)
> **Type:** Year-long membership program (weekly calls, live events, Q&A)
> **Date Range:** March 2019 -- January 2021
> **Status:** COMPLETE
> **Perspective:** Justin Goff contributions (see stefan-georgi/SOURCE-CA.md for SG perspective)

### JG Primary Speaker Sections

Justin Goff was primary or co-primary speaker across multiple sections, with dominant presence in offer engineering, conversion optimization, business strategy, and compliance:

| Section | JG Role | Key JG Contributions |

| 08 -- FB Compliance | Co-primary (50%) | Compliant offer framing, account survival, risk mitigation |
| 11-13 -- Live Events | Co-primary | In-person masterminds, advanced offer sessions |

## KEY FRAMEWORKS EXTRACTED

```
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  OLAF METHOD (Offer, Length, Opening, Format)              ^[FW-JG-001]     |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|  The 4 levers that determine if a sales piece will convert:                  |
|                                                                              |
|  O - OFFER:   Is the offer a "hell yes"? Does it pass the 3 criteria?       |
|  L - LENGTH:  Is the copy the right length for the awareness level?          |
|  A - OPENING: Does the opening hook & qualify in the first 10 seconds?       |
|  F - FORMAT:  Is the format matched to the traffic temperature?              |
|                                                                              |
|  Primary application: upsell optimization (pushed conversion to 44%+)        |
|  Secondary application: front-end offer diagnostics                          |
|                                                                              |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  HELL YES OFFER                                            ^[FW-JG-005]     |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|  3 criteria that make an offer irresistible:                                 |
|                                                                              |
|  1. DESIRE -- Does the market desperately WANT this? (not "need")            |
|  2. BELIEVABILITY -- Do they believe THIS solution will work for THEM?       |
|  3. EXCLUSIVITY -- Can they get this ONLY from you?                          |
|                                                                              |
|  All 3 must be present. Missing any one = weak offer.                        |
|  Validation test: 7/10 target market people must say "hell yes"              |
|                                                                              |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  BIG IDEA FORMULA                                          ^[FW-JG-003]     |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|  Framework for finding the "big idea" that drives an entire campaign:        |
|                                                                              |
|  1. What is the ONE thing the market wants most?                             |
|  2. What is the ONE mechanism that delivers it?                              |
|  3. What is the ONE emotion that drives the buying decision?                 |
|  4. Combine: [Emotion] + [Mechanism] + [Outcome] = Big Idea                 |
|                                                                              |
|  "Every winning campaign has one big idea. Not three. One."                  |
|                                                                              |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  3 UNIVERSAL UPSELL TYPES                                  ^[FW-JG-008]     |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|  Type 1: COMPLEMENTARY -- Enhances the main purchase                         |
|  Type 2: MORE OF -- Same category, higher quantity or duration               |
|  Type 3: PREMIUM TIER -- Upgraded version with more access/features          |
|                                                                              |
|  For info products, "Done For You" consistently wins as upsell #1.           |
|  Every upsell sequence should use all 3 types in optimal order.              |
|                                                                              |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  8 BIGGEST MISTAKES ON COLD TRAFFIC                        ^[FW-JG-012]     |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|  Diagnostic checklist for failing cold traffic campaigns:                    |
|                                                                              |
|  1. Weak offer (no "hell yes" reaction)                                      |
|  2. Wrong audience targeting                                                 |
|  3. Opening doesn't hook in <10 seconds                                      |
|  4. Too much copy for awareness level                                        |
|  5. No unique mechanism                                                      |
|  6. No social proof early enough                                             |
|  7. CTA is unclear or buried                                                 |
|  8. No upsell sequence (leaving money on table)                              |
|                                                                              |
|  "Fix these 8 before you blame the traffic source."                          |
|                                                                              |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  ANNUAL PLANNING FRAMEWORK                                 ^[FW-JG-010]     |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|  Reverse-engineer annual revenue goal into executable quarters:              |
|                                                                              |
|  1. Set annual revenue target                                                |
|  2. Break into quarterly milestones                                          |
|  3. Identify 3 needle movers per quarter                                     |
|  4. Assign metrics and owners                                                |
|  5. Monthly check-in: on track or pivot?                                     |
|  6. Kill anything not moving the needle                                      |
|                                                                              |
|  "Most people plan features. Winners plan revenue backwards."                |
|                                                                              |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  NEEDLE MOVER TESTING PRIORITY                             ^[FW-JG-015]     |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|  What to test first when optimizing a funnel:                                |
|                                                                              |
|  Priority 1: OFFER (highest leverage)                                        |
|  Priority 2: OPENING (first 10 seconds / 300 words)                          |
|  Priority 3: UPSELL SEQUENCE (AOV impact)                                    |
|  Priority 4: HEADLINE / SUBJECT LINE                                         |
|  Priority 5: BODY COPY (lowest leverage)                                     |
|                                                                              |
|  "Most people start at #5. Winners start at #1."                             |
|                                                                              |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

## DNA ELEMENTS EXTRACTED

### Summary by Layer

| Layer | Count | Key Content |
|-------|-------|-------------|
| L1 -- Philosophies | 28 | Offer-first thinking, complexity kills, data over opinions, AOV-centric business model |
| L2 -- Mental Models | 19 | Needle Mover Thinking, PaleoSweets vs PaleoPlenty, 10-Star Filter, 3 Upsell Types |
| L3 -- Heuristics | 35 | Cold traffic benchmarks, AOV targets, testing priority, email frequency, kill timelines |
| L4 -- Frameworks | 17 | OLAF, Hell Yes Offer, Big Idea Formula, 3 Universal Upsells, Annual Planning, 8 Mistakes |
| L5 -- Methodologies | 11 | Hell Yes Construction, Cold Traffic Diagnostic, Annual Planning Reverse Eng, Upsell Build-Out |
| **TOTAL** | **110** | |

### Top Philosophies (L1)

| ID | Philosophy |
|----|-----------|
| FIL-JG-001 | The offer is everything -- if it doesn't make them say "hell yes," copy won't save it |
| FIL-JG-002 | AOV is the real business model -- front-end is the entry point, upsells are the business |
| FIL-JG-003 | Plan the upsell before you build the product |
| FIL-JG-004 | No amount of copy polish fixes a bad offer |
| FIL-JG-005 | Complexity kills businesses -- find the needle mover and ignore everything else |
| FIL-JG-006 | Revenue per email, not open rate -- vanity metrics kill businesses |
| FIL-JG-008 | Cold traffic is the ultimate validator |
| FIL-JG-009 | People buy what they WANT (cookies), not what they NEED (fennel chicken) |
| FIL-JG-015 | Plan backwards from revenue, not forward from activity |
| FIL-JG-019 | Test the offer before the copy |
| FIL-JG-020 | Your opinion about what works is worth exactly $0 -- data decides |
| FIL-JG-025 | Speed of implementation beats perfection |

### Top Heuristics (L3 -- Quantitative)

| ID | Heuristic |
|----|-----------|
| HEUR-JG-001 | Offer must get "hell yes" reaction in 10 seconds or it's weak |
| HEUR-JG-005 | Cold traffic opening must hook in <10 seconds |
| HEUR-JG-010 | If it fails on cold traffic, fix the offer (not the copy) |
| HEUR-JG-012 | Order form bump target: 30-40% take rate minimum |
| HEUR-JG-015 | AOV optimization > CVR optimization for profitability |
| HEUR-JG-018 | 3 needle movers per quarter max -- more = dilution |
| HEUR-JG-020 | Upsell sequence should add 40-80% to front-end AOV |
| HEUR-JG-025 | Test offer first, then opening, then upsells, then body |
| HEUR-JG-028 | Monthly revenue check: if <85% of target, pivot immediately |
| HEUR-JG-030 | Reactivation emails to dead list: 3-email "wake up" sequence |
| HEUR-JG-033 | Email promo frequency: 1 major promotion per 2 weeks max |
| HEUR-JG-035 | Kill any project not moving needle within 30 days |

### Top Methodologies (L5)

| ID | Methodology | Steps |
|----|------------|-------|
| MET-JG-001 | Hell Yes Offer Construction | Desire audit -> Believability stack -> Exclusivity engineering -> Test |
| MET-JG-003 | 8 Biggest Mistakes Cold Traffic Diagnostic | Run 8-point checklist -> Identify failure point -> Fix -> Relaunch |
| MET-JG-005 | Annual Planning Reverse Engineering | Annual target -> Quarterly milestones -> 3 needle movers -> Execute |
| MET-JG-007 | Upsell Sequence Build-Out | Map 3 types -> Sequence order -> Price anchoring -> Test -> Optimize |
| MET-JG-009 | Email Reactivation Protocol | Segment dead list -> 3-email "wake up" -> Re-engage -> Promo sequence |
| MET-JG-011 | Offer Appeal Scoring | Score Desire (1-10) + Believability (1-10) + Exclusivity (1-10) -> Go/NoGo |

## PATRIOT HEALTH ALLIANCE CASE STUDY

```
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  PATRIOT HEALTH ALLIANCE -- THE $23M+ CASE STUDY                            |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|  Throughout Copy Accelerator, Goff uses his Patriot Health Alliance          |
|  experience as the primary teaching case study. Key lessons:                 |
|                                                                              |
|  1. OFFER APPEAL: PaleoSweets (cookies) outsold PaleoPlenty (healthy        |
|     recipes) by 200:1 on the same traffic. Proof that WANT > NEED.          |
|                                                                              |
|  2. FRONT-END ECONOMICS: Front-end often at breakeven. 100% of profit       |
|     came from upsell sequences and back-end offers.                          |
|                                                                              |
|  3. AOV ARCHITECTURE: Built a multi-step upsell sequence that added         |
|     40-80% to front-end AOV consistently.                                    |
|                                                                              |
|  4. COLD TRAFFIC SCALING: Validated everything on cold traffic first.        |
|     If it didn't work cold, it wasn't worth pursuing.                       |
|                                                                              |
|  5. OFFER LIFECYCLE: Learned to kill offers after 8-10 months of            |
|     mediocre performance rather than trying to optimize them forever.        |
|                                                                              |
|  6. COMPLIANCE: Navigated health supplement compliance on Facebook           |
|     while maintaining conversion rates.                                      |
|                                                                              |
|  Revenue: $23M+ over the life of the business                               |
|                                                                              |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

## PROCESSING NOTES

### Extraction Methodology

1. **Scan:** All 759 files scanned for content (wc -c, not wc -l)
2. **Filter:** 360 empty/stub files excluded (PDF covers, 0-16 bytes)
3. **Speaker Attribution:** Elements attributed to JG based on speaker identification in transcriptions
4. **Extract:** 110 DNA elements extracted across 5 layers
5. **Validate:** Cross-referenced with Patriot Health Alliance case studies for consistency
6. **Assign:** Elements assigned to appropriate DNA layers (L1-L5)

### Quality Notes

- Section 05 (Conversion Boosters) is Goff's strongest section -- OLAF Method deep-dive
- Section 06 (Copywriting Business) reflects his unique "copywriter-to-CEO" perspective
- Patriot Health Alliance case studies are woven throughout multiple sections
- Guest contributions (23 elements) were attributed separately and are NOT included in JG's 110
- Facebook compliance content (Section 08) is time-sensitive -- policies change

### Known Gaps

| Gap | Impact | Mitigation |
|-----|--------|------------|
| PDF-only content not transcribed | ~360 files inaccessible | Focus on 399 content files (sufficient coverage) |
| Some JG/SG attribution unclear in Q&A | A few elements may be mis-attributed | Cross-referenced with known JG topics (offer, AOV, upsells) |
| No standalone JG program | All content from shared CA | JG perspective isolated by speaker and topic |
